blocks.text: was already watching the house next door when she reached for her bag. She didn't say anything about it.
grabbed both bags without cataloguing anything. He was going to try.
went to the kitchen first. The layout was good. He could work with it.
picked up both bags and came inside without saying anything. The water was good though. He'd give it that.

blocks.content: The sand was still warm from the afternoon sun — fine, pale, the kind that gets into everything. Julian had taken his shoes off at the bottom of the deck stairs because Mara had already done it and left hers on the third step.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: When's the last time you had sand between your toes?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I don't remember.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: That's the whole problem right there.

A jetski cut a white line across the water a quarter mile out, gone in ten seconds. Further south, two sailboats moved slowly along the horizon, their sails small and very white against the darkening blue.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Those boats have been out since at least noon. Look at the angle they're running.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Are you navigating them?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I'm just looking.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Good. Keep doing that.

There was something about being next to water. The sound of it — continuous, push and pull — made it hard to hold onto whatever I'd been carrying out of the car. I wasn't sure I wanted to examine that too closely.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Feel that?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Yeah.
They walked south along the waterline. The wet sand was harder underfoot and the ocean came up past their ankles every few waves. Mara walked straight into the first one.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Oh — okay. It went straight up to my thighs. I was not ready for that.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: You walked directly at it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I was looking at the boats.
Julian stepped in sideways, tracking the next wave. It caught him mid-shin and kept coming.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Okay. That's more pull than I expected.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Ha.
They were passing the neighboring house when the screen door swung open and a tall man with an easy stride came down toward the water, one hand raised like he already knew them.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Hey! You're the neighbors, right? I'm Marcus. We've been watching to see who showed up.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Julian.
They shook hands — firm, easy. Marcus had that thing where he looked at you like you were already someone he liked.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Mara. It's so good to meet you.
She hugged him like they'd already been friends for ten minutes. Marcus laughed, surprised, and hugged her back.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Okay! We like these neighbors. Come on, let me get the rest of them.
He turned and called back toward the house: "Yo — the neighbors are out here, come meet them." A screen door banged. Then another.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: Cade. You survive the drive?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Mostly. You?

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: Four hours. GPS took me through a town that I'm pretty sure doesn't exist anymore.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: That's awful. We had three and a half.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: I hate you both.
Dean came down the steps with the kind of posture that didn't change based on who was around. He shook Julian's hand once, solid.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: Dean. What do you do?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Consulting. Corporate side — fraud analysis, mostly. You?

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: Architect. Commercial projects, mostly downtown Charlotte.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: Sophie — hey, come here.
She was already halfway down the steps, phone in one hand.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: Hi! Oh — I love that top. Where is that from?

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Thank you! It's from this little boutique in Georgetown — and can I just say, that whole look you've got going is absolutely the right energy for this weekend.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: Stop it. Interior design — you train yourself to think about these things and then you can't turn it off.
Mara hugged her. Sophie, mid-sentence, hugged back just as warmly. Dean watched this happen with the expression of a man who was not even slightly surprised.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: She's mine, by the way. Just so you have the full picture.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: And I claim him. For the record.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: That's a good match.

Nadia came down last — unhurried, pleasant smile. She shook Mara's hand and then leaned in for a brief hug, the kind that said she was comfortable with people without needing to be loud about it. Julian shook her hand, she held it a half second, looked at him once with calm dark eyes, and let go.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Nadia. Good timing on the weekend — weather held better than I thought it would.

At the edge of the group, a woman with short dark hair stood slightly back from the rest. She'd been there since they walked up. Someone said her name — Wren — and she gave a small nod, like that covered it.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: Okay wait — are you two together?

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Oh — no. We work together. Just two colleagues who needed a break.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: No offense, but that's not what it looks like.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I was going to say the same thing and I decided to be polite.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: She dragged me out here. That's the whole story.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I did. He needed it.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: That sounds like a couple.
Everyone laughed. Mara shook her head, smiling at the sand.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Listen — come over tonight. Fire's going by eight, we'll have food, it'll be a whole thing. You'd be doing us a favor. Dean already ran out of people to debate.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: That is not accurate.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: It's a little accurate.
Mara glanced over at Julian. She didn't even finish the look.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Yeah. We'll be there.
Marcus grinned like that was exactly what he'd been hoping for.

blocks.prompt: He said yes without thinking about it. What just happened?

options.label: He knows why. Mara was right there and he knew she'd want to go.
The fire. The group. He just wanted to be in it.
He didn't think about it. That was the whole point.
